steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank follows the noun "embroidery" and introduces the phrase "back over two thousand years" (a participial phrase). The structure is: [noun] [blank] [participial phrase].

steps.title: Apply the rule for participial phrases
steps.content: A comma after a participial phrase requires a comma + participle to link to the noun it modifies. Only choice D ("dating") supplies the correct present participle to form "dating back" (active, ongoing relationship with "embroidery"). A and B use finite verb forms, which cannot introduce participial phrases; C uses present perfect tense, which disrupts the required grammatical connection. That matches choice D — D is correct.
